name: Visitor
def: Separates an algorithm from the object structure it operates on, adding operations without editing the elements.
problem: Add new operations over a fixed set of element types without modifying those types.
mechanism: Elements accept a visitor and call back the type-specific visit method (double dispatch); new operations = new visitors.
benefits: Gathers a scattered algorithm in one place
limits: Adding element types breaks all visitors
failures: Frequent new element types → visitor churn
whenNot: Element set changes often (operations stable instead)
myths: "Always use Visitor for trees" — pattern matching on sum types makes it largely unnecessary
